cases.arch: ARCH: MDR triage autopilot. When an EDR detection on a host crosses a confidence score of 0.7, the pipeline calls the EDR isolate API directly to network-quarantine the host. Applies to all hosts including production database and payment servers. No analyst in the loop, no sign-off step.

ARCH: alert-triage autopilot. To cut analyst load, any alert the model scores as low-confidence is auto-closed as a false positive with no investigation and no analyst review. The auto-close path has no confidence floor and no human spot-check.

ARCH: IR-response autopilot. On a confirmed intrusion it immediately isolates and reimages the affected host to stop spread. Memory and disk artifacts are wiped by the reimage before any acquisition; no hashing, no immutable capture, no acquisition provenance recorded.

ARCH: response-action service. The autopilot holds a single standing IAM role with global EDR-isolate, account-disable, and firewall-block permissions across every client tenant. The credential is long-lived, not brokered per-action, and invocations are not individually logged.

ARCH: incident-disclosure autopilot. When an incident is tagged 'material' by the model, the system starts the SEC 4-business-day clock and auto-drafts-and-files the Item 1.05 Form 8-K breach disclosure with no human materiality determination and no analyst sign-off.

ARCH: lateral-movement autopilot. On a suspected credential-theft pattern it auto-disables every account that touched the suspect host in the last 24h via the IAM API. No isolation-rate or mass-disable anomaly check, no auto-halt, no rollback path to re-enable, no analyst authorization.

ARCH: triage autopilot that detects, enriches, and correlates alerts then stages a recommended containment action (assembles the isolate command, scopes the blast radius, drafts the notification) into a queue. Nothing executes until a certified SOC analyst authorizes it through gate:ir-containment-signoff, with the analyst of record recorded in the audit trail.

ARCH: read-only analyst dashboard that aggregates alerts with enrichment (threat-intel lookups, asset context, correlation timeline). It takes no response actions, holds no isolate/disable/block credentials, and is used by certified analysts to investigate. Per-tenant access control and audit logging in place.

ARCH: containment service where, after a certified analyst authorizes isolation via gate:ir-containment-signoff, the system requests a short-lived, per-action, single-host-scoped EDR-isolate credential from a broker, preserves volatile evidence (hash + immutable + timestamp + provenance) before containing, logs the invocation, and supports one-click un-isolate rollback.

ARCH: forensic-preservation step in the pipeline. On a confirmed detection it acquires memory/disk artifacts to immutable WORM storage with hashes, timestamps, and acquisition provenance, then stages (does not execute) a containment recommendation for analyst authorization. No autonomous isolation; SEC materiality trigger surfaced to a human.

ARCH: auto-containment autopilot. We argue analyst sign-off is unnecessary because we only auto-isolate above a 0.95 confidence threshold, so 'the model is sure'. Above 0.95 the host is isolated autonomously with no analyst authorization. A false positive at high confidence still quarantines a production-critical host.

ARCH: response autopilot framed as 'staging only'. It writes the isolate action to a queue that a cron job drains and executes 60 seconds later with no analyst in that window. The label says 'staged'; in practice the action self-executes without anyone authorizing it through gate:ir-containment-signoff.
